title: Listening from American Accent
description: Dictation practice built from real Practice Sentences and Sentence Pairs audio.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 4–5)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: The employees agreed to meet at eight fifteen.
Don’t keep the TV near the heater.
It’s extremely easy to cheat when the teacher isn’t here.
Please speak to Peter about the employee meeting.
Steve will reread the email before he leaves.
/I/ AS IN SIT

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 4–5)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Kim will visit her big sister Linda in Virginia.
In the beginning it was difficult for Jim to quit drinking.
The Smiths invited him to an informal dinner.
This city has an interesting history.
When did Bill Clinton visit the Middle East?

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 6)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: She complained about her weight but ate the cake anyway.
Jake hates waiting for trains and planes.
It rains and hails in April and May.
I will stay in the game even though it’s late.
My neighbor from Spain moved away today.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 7)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Without some extra effort, you will never excel.
Jenny and her friend had eggs for breakfast.
I expect this session to end at ten.
On the seventh of February, the weather was wet.
I see my best friend Fred every seven days.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 8)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: This is your last chance to give me an accurate answer.
Sam sat at the back of the math class.
Danny had a salad and a sandwich in the cafeteria.
Nancy has a bad attitude in her Spanish class.
Kathy would rather study acting at the national academy.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 9)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Ronald is confident that he got the job.
Scott goes to a lot of rock concerts.
The doctor operated in the modern hospital.
Bob will probably lock the office.
He’s got a lot of dollars in his pocket.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 11–12)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Your younger brother doesn’t trust us.
What country does he come from?
I had another fun summer in London.
I don’t have much stuff in the trunk of my truck.
I love the sunny summer months.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 11–12)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: The audience applauded even though the talk was awful.
His small daughter thought that Santa Claus would come in August.
I saw your mother-in-law in the mall.
He bought an automobile at the auction last fall.
This sauce is awesome, Paula!

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 13)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: We both hope it’s going to snow.
Oh, no! Don’t open the window! It’s cold.
Do you want to go bowling or roller skating?
I chose a bowl of soup, potatoes, roast beef, and a soda.
I don’t know if Joan smokes.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 14–15)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Would you help me look for my book?
The sugar cookies taste good.
The butcher is a good cook.
He would read the book if he could.
Butch visited his old neighborhood in Brooklyn.
/u/ AS IN TOO

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 14–15)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: The new roof was installed in June.
I drink fruit juice and eat a lot of soup.
Your blue shoes are really cool.
I need proof that you’re telling the truth.
The statue on the avenue is truly beautiful.

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 16–17)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: I will work during the third term.
They served turkey for dinner.
Her purple shirt is dirty.
She gave birth to a third girl.
It’s not worth worrying about another birthday.
/aɪ/ AS IN TIME

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 16–17)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Why is the price so high for that design?
The wildfire started on Friday night.
He was tired after hiking for five hours.
It’s a nine-hour drive to Iowa.
We had lime pie and dry white wine.
/aʊ/ AS IN HOUSE

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 18)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
tracks.audio: /american-accent-audio/063 Practice Sentences-I doubt that the clown.mp3
sentences: I doubt that the clown will say something profound.
There are flowers all around the house.
Is that your spouse in the brown blouse?
The clouds behind the mountain will bring showers.
The brown cow is near the fountain.
/ɔɪ/ AS IN BOY

tracks.title: Chapter One — Practice Sentences (p. 18)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: He destroyed the poison by flushing it down the toilet.

I was disappointed with Joy’s choice.
Why is Floyd avoiding Roy?

tracks.heading: Sentence Pairs for Practice
sentences: Don’t think about the pest.

Send it carefully.

I need a new pen.

tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Every member of my family is left handed.

sentences: Are you a fool?

tracks.title: Chapter Two — Practice Sentences (p. 31)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: It’s your duty to produce it by Tuesday.
Those students like iTunes and YouTube.
May I introduce you to my tutor?

tracks.title: Chapter Two — Sentences for Practice (p. 32)
tracks.heading: Sentences for Practice
sentences: What were the first words that she learned?
I will learn the German verbs by Thursday.

tracks.title: Chapter Two — Practice Sentences (p. 32)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Take the stairs in case of fire.
The employer is hiring and firing.

tracks.title: Chapter Three — Practice Sentences (p. 34–36)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: voiceless
voiced
My wallet is in the back.
My wallet is in the bag.
I saw five bucks on the floor.
I saw five bugs on the floor.
He has blue ice.
He has blue eyes.
I heard about the lice.
I heard about the lies.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 39–41)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
tracks.audio: /american-accent-audio/126 Practice Sentences-I might not do that-TIP.mp3
sentences: I might not do that.
It's not that great.
He built that website last night.
It felt quite hot in Vermont.
What?! That can't be right!
Matt went out for a bite to eat.
That apartment felt quite hot.
If you eat out every night you'll get fat.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 39–41)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: I will shorten the curtain.
He has eaten the rotten food.
I’m certain that it was written in Britain.
I’ve already forgotten the sentence.
That cotton blouse has buttons.
Martin Luther King and Bill Clinton are famous Americans.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 43)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: I’ll eat it a little later.
I bought an auto battery for forty dollars.
Peter wrote  a better letter.
I’d better go  to the meeting at eleven.
He met her at a computer store in Seattle.
It’s a pity that he’s getting fatter and fatter.
Tell the waiter to bring it a little later.
He bought a lot of bottles of water.
Betty’s knitting a little sweater for her daughter.
It’ll be better if you heat it before you eat it.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences for “Fast d” (p. 44)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences for “Fast d”
sentences: I already added it.
Adam will edit the middle part.
Those products are made in Canada.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 46–47)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Did you go to his graduation?
Would you take our picture?
Why can’t you be punctual?
Don’t you like nature?
Actually, this is a fortunate situation.
You’re adventurous, aren’t you?
Why won’t you do it gradually?
Can’t you change your schedule?

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 60–61)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Five of David’s relatives live in Vienna.
Steve and Vivian will come over at eleven.

Twelve of us drove to the river near Vegas.

I’ve been given a favorable evaluation.

They’ve never believed my viewpoint.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 62)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Beverly is very busy developing her new business.
Vince loves basketball and baseball.
Ben drove to Las Vegas in his black Volvo.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 65–66)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Victor’s wife Vicky was very wise.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 67–68)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: A dishwasher washes dishes.
A bus driver drives buses.

tracks.title: Chapter Four — Practice Sentences (p. 69)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Don’t bring the wrong rings to the wedding.

tracks.title: Chapter Five — Practice Sentences (p. 80–83)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Underline the stressed syllables in the verbs and nouns in bold letters. To check your answers,
listen to the audio.
The singer wants to record a new record.
The drug addict is addicted to heroin.
He insulted me with a rude insult.
I would like to present all of the present members.
This permit permits you to park your car here.
They protested in the protest.
Do you object to this object?
The convict was convicted again.
I suspect that they caught the suspect.
They are going to contest the results of the contest.

tracks.title: Chapter Five — Sentence Pairs for Practice (p. 85)
tracks.heading: Sentence Pairs for Practice
sentences: He likes politics.
He wants to be a politician.
I love photography.
Do you take a lot of photographs?
He studied economy.
He is an economical shopper.
Do you know that family?
Yes, they’re familiar to me.
He is a very good mechanic.
He is fixing the mechanism.
Their opinions vary.
There is a variety of opinions in the room.
We celebrate Memorial Day.
It’s in memory of the veterans.
Do you know the origin of your name?
No, it’s pretty original.
He is a registered Democrat.
He watched the democratic debate on TV.
It is not necessary to do that.
I don’t necessarily agree.
He likes to compete.
He’s always been very competitive.

tracks.heading: Practice Sentences

sentences: He’s got big blue eyes and short black hair.

tracks.title: Chapter Six — Practice Sentences (p. 93)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: They had a good time playing football.

tracks.heading: Sentence Pairs for Practice

sentences: The car was tuned up.

sentences: He arrived at LAX at 8:25 AM.

tracks.title: Chapter Six — Practice Sentences (p. 98–99)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: I like bacon.

It's black.

That's good.
That's a good idea.
It's hot.
It's a hot day.
I need it.
I need to go.
I need to go home.
I need to go home at five o’clock.
I saw him.
I saw the man.
I saw the man you told me about.
He drove it.
He drove the car.
He drove the car he bought yesterday.
He drove the car that he bought from his friend.

tracks.title: Chapter Six — Practice Sentences (p. 98–99)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: reduced.
I’ll call you.
I saw him.
I’ll wait for you.
I have to go.
It’s nice to meet you.

tracks.title: Chapter Six — Practice Sentences (p. 98–99)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: stressed nouns:
reduced pronouns:
He told John.
He told him.
I like that car.
I like it.
I need a job.
I need it.

tracks.title: Chapter Six — Practice Sentences (p. 100–103)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Wait!
I’ll wait for you.
I can wait for you.
I am waiting for you.
I’ll be waiting for you.
I’ve been waiting for you.
I could’ve waited for you.
I could’ve been waiting for you.
I’ll wait for you in the car.
I should’ve been waiting for you in the car.
Tell her.
He’ll tell her.
He’ll be telling her.
He didn’t tell her.
He should have told her.
He should’ve been telling her.
He didn’t tell his wife.
He should’ve been telling his wife.
He didn’t tell his wife about the situation.
He should’ve been telling his wife about the situation.
I bought it.
I bought a watch.
I bought a new watch.
I bought a new gold watch.
I bought a new gold watch for him.
I bought a new gold watch for his birthday.
I bought a new gold watch for his thirtieth birthday.
I would have bought a new gold watch for his thirtieth birthday.
He lost it.
He lost the money.
I think he lost the money.
I think he lost the money again.
I think he lost the money that I gave him.
He might have lost the money that I gave him.
I think he might have lost the money that I gave him.

tracks.title: Chapter Seven — Practice Sentences (p. 109–110)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Did he work yesterday?
Does he know about it?
Can you call me at five?
Is it good?
Is that it?
Excuse me?
Really?

tracks.title: Chapter Seven — Sentence Pairs for Practice (p. 111)
tracks.heading: Sentence Pairs for Practice
tracks.audio: /american-accent-audio/331 Sentence Pairs For Practice-Do you teach.mp3
sentences: Do you teach?
What do you teach?

When did you see the movie?
Do you know that guy?
How do you know that guy?
Did you buy the car?
Where did you buy the car?
Do you work there?

tracks.title: Chapter Seven — Sentence Pairs for Practice (p. 111)
tracks.heading: Sentence Pairs for Practice

sentences: I bought the book.
I bought the book, but I didn’t read it.
I finished school.
When I finished school, I moved to New York.
I’ll study hard.
If I study hard, I’ll get an A.
I’m going inside.
I’m going inside, to get something to drink.

tracks.title: Chapter Seven — Practice Sentences (p. 112–113)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
tracks.audio: /american-accent-audio/335 Practice Sentences-As a matter of fact.mp3
sentences: As a matter of fact, I do know the answer.
As far as I’m concerned, you did great.
Actually, it was pretty good.

If you don’t mind, I’d like to close the window.

tracks.title: Chapter Seven — Practice Sentences (p. 112–113)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: I like football, basketball, tennis, and golf.
I’m taking math, biology, French, and history.
I left work, came home, and had dinner.
I need milk, apples, eggs, and sugar.
“I learned law so well, the day I graduated I sued the college, won the case, and got my tuition back.”

tracks.title: Chapter Eight — Practice Sentences (p. 120)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: Both things are from me.
Stop playing and help Paul.

I’m married, and I’m miserable.

tracks.title: Chapter Eight — Practice Sentences (p. 122–125)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: I ate out.
Go on.
They agree.
I know it.
May I come in?
So awesome!
I’ll buy it.

tracks.title: Chapter Eight — Practice Sentences (p. 131)
tracks.heading: Practice Sentences
sentences: If I’d known it was your birthday, I would’ve gotten you a present.
If you hadn’t been driving so fast, you wouldn’ve gotten a ticket.
If the weather’d been warmer, we would’ve gone to the park.
If he’d been more careful, he wouldn’ve had an accident.
I would’ve passed the test if I’d studied more.
If it hadn’t rained we would have canceled the picnic.
It would have been more fun if there’d been more people at the party.
I would have called you if you’d given me your number.
If they’d come on time, they would not have missed their flight.
She would not have known if you hadn’t told her.
